inclusion_criteria: Population: Adults (≥18 years) with a formal diagnosis of Mild Cognitive Impairment (any subtype) or mild/early-stage Alzheimer's Disease, according to established criteria (e.g., NIA-AA, DSM-5, Petersen).

Intervention: Application of a non-invasive brain stimulation technique, including repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (rTMS, iTBS, cTBS), transcranial Direct Current Stimulation (tDCS), or transcranial Alternating Current Stimulation (tACS).
Comparison: Sham stimulation, no intervention, usual care, waiting list, or an active control group (e.g., different NIBS parameters).
Outcomes: At least one primary or secondary outcome of interest is reported (e.g., global cognition, safety, ADLs, neuropsychiatric symptoms).
Study Design: Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT), Controlled Clinical Trial (CCT), or randomized crossover trial with an adequate washout period.
Publication Date: Published between January 1, 2000 and December 31, 2025.
Language: Full text available in English or Chinese.
exclusion_criteria: Population: Healthy controls, subjective cognitive decline without MCI diagnosis, moderate-to-severe dementia, or other dementia etiologies (e.g., vascular, frontotemporal, Lewy body).

Intervention: Invasive stimulation (e.g., DBS, VNS), electroconvulsive therapy (ECT), or studies where the effect of NIBS cannot be isolated from a concurrent active intervention (e.g., NIBS + cognitive training vs. cognitive training alone).
Study Design: Non-controlled trials, observational studies, case reports/series, reviews, meta-analyses, protocols, conference abstracts, or animal/in-vitro studies.
Population: Studies exclusively on pediatric or adolescent populations (<18 years).
